title: Define a Print Job Model
text: Define a Print Job Model You must define a print job model before you can print data from Form components. Note: Printing data in the Uniface IDE is only possible in the editors available from the More Editors menu. In the Uniface IDE, choose Print Setup from the main menu ( ≡ ). Clear the Print Job Model field. Then click >> to display a list of available models. Click New to open the Define Print Job Model dialog. You can also select a model and click Edit to change an existing model. Specify the name and other details of the print job model. At minimum, you must specify a value in Name and the device translation table in Device Type . Options include: Name —print job model name Device Type —device translation table to use for this print job model Print Queue —destination of the printed output Print Options —additional operating system options Device Mode —predefined page formatting for a specific Device Type Margins —margins of the printed output, expressed in terms of lines and characters Print using colors Print black on white Clear the Device Type field or enter a profile (for example P_Data * ). Click >> to display the list of available device translation tables. Select a table and click OK . Click OK to save the print job model. Example: Typical Print Job Model for Windows For example, the following print job model uses the enhanced printing device translation table for Windows ( Device Type : P_MSWINX ) and invokes the Windows Print dialog box so that the user can configure and control printing ( Print Queue : printman ). Related Links Print Job Models A print job model is a named collection of printer attributes that determine how and where Uniface forms or reports are printed, including details of the device type, margins, and so on. A print job model must be available before you can print. Define a Logical Printer On Windows, you can define a logical printer, which is a named collection of printer settings for a physical printer. You can define more than one logical printer for the same physical printer. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
